name: auth.clientCredentials
description: OAuth 2.0 Client Credentials flow (RFC 6749 Section 4.4) for service principal / machine-to-machine authentication. Used by CI/CD pipelines, automated agents, and backend services that need to authenticate without user interaction.

client_id.description: Service principal client identifier (UUID or string). Obtain from 'guideai service-principals create' or organization admin console.

client_secret.description: Service principal client secret. IMPORTANT: Never hardcode - use environment variables or secrets manager.

scopes.description: Requested OAuth scopes. Must be subset of allowed_scopes configured on the service principal. If omitted, grants all allowed scopes.

status.description: Authentication result: 'authorized' on success, 'error' on failure.

service_principal_id.description: UUID of the authenticated service principal. Present only on success.

org_id.description: UUID of the organization the service principal belongs to. OPTIONAL - only present if SP is associated with an org. Service principals can exist without an organization.

name.description: Human-readable name of the service principal.

scopes.description: Granted scopes for this session. May be less than requested if service principal has limited allowed_scopes.

role.description: Role assigned to this service principal (e.g., 'service', 'admin', 'readonly').

expires_in.description: Session lifetime in seconds. Default 86400 (24 hours) for service principals.

error.description: Human-readable error message. Present only on failure.

error_code.description: Machine-readable error code. Present only on failure.

notes: NEVER log or expose client_secret values
Use environment variables or secrets managers for credentials
Rotate secrets periodically and immediately on suspected compromise
Monitor failed authentication attempts for brute force detection
